days.title: Calorie Counting
days.essence: Sum calories within each blank-line-delimited elf group, then take the max; part 2 wants the top three summed. Pure warmup centred on splitting the input on double newlines.

days.title: Rock Paper Scissors
days.essence: Score a rock-paper-scissors strategy guide by table lookup; part 2 reinterprets the second column as the desired outcome so you must derive the hand to play. Easy lookup-driven scoring.

days.title: Rucksack Reorganization
days.essence: Find the single item shared between the two halves of each rucksack, then the badge common to each group of three lines, summing letter priorities. Straightforward set-intersection over strings.

days.title: Camp Cleanup
days.essence: For each pair of numeric ranges, count full containment (part 1) and any overlap (part 2). A clean interval-comparison exercise.

days.title: Supply Stacks
days.essence: Parse a fixed-width ASCII diagram of crate stacks and replay move instructions; part 2 moves crates as an ordered batch instead of one at a time. Difficulty is in the fiddly column parsing and stack bookkeeping.

days.title: Tuning Trouble
days.essence: Find the first sliding window of N fully distinct characters in the datastream (4 for part 1, 14 for part 2). A trivial windowed-uniqueness scan.

days.title: No Space Left On Device
days.essence: Reconstruct a directory tree from a terminal session of cd/ls commands and compute recursive directory sizes; part 2 picks the smallest directory whose deletion frees enough space. Building the tree from the command log is the real work.

days.title: Treetop Tree House
days.essence: On a grid of tree heights, count trees visible from outside along the four cardinal lines; part 2 maximises a scenic score from viewing distances in each direction. Grid sight-line scanning, here done by rotating the grid four times.

days.title: Rope Bridge
days.essence: Simulate a rope head's moves while each knot follows the one ahead by the sign of their offset, counting unique tail positions; part 2 extends the rope from 2 knots to 10. Faithful step-by-step simulation of the follow rule.

days.title: Cathode-Ray Tube
days.essence: Run a tiny CPU with noop/addx (two-cycle) instructions, sampling signal strength at fixed cycles; part 2 uses the register as a sprite position to render letters on a 40x6 CRT. A cycle-accurate register-machine simulation.

days.title: Monkey in the Middle
days.essence: Simulate monkeys inspecting and throwing items by per-monkey operations and divisibility tests to compute monkey business. Part 1 divides worry by 3, but part 2 removes that and runs 10000 rounds, so worry levels must be kept bounded modulo the product of all divisors (the user solved only part 1).

days.title: Hill Climbing Algorithm
days.essence: BFS the shortest climb over a heightmap where you may step up at most one level; part 2 finds the shortest path starting from any lowest-elevation cell. Classic unweighted shortest-path on a grid graph.

days.title: Distress Signal
days.essence: Compare nested integer/list packets with a recursive ordering rule (wrapping bare ints in lists); part 2 sorts all packets with that comparator and multiplies the divider indices. A recursive deep-compare over nested structures.

days.title: Regolith Reservoir
days.essence: Drop grains of falling sand that settle by the down/down-left/down-right rule until they fall into the abyss; part 2 adds an infinite floor and counts until the source is plugged. Direct grain-by-grain grid simulation.

days.title: Beacon Exclusion Zone
days.essence: Each sensor excludes a Manhattan-distance diamond; part 1 counts covered cells on one row. Part 2 must locate the single uncovered point in a 4-million-wide square, which requires merging coverage intervals per row rather than enumerating cells (the user's part 1 enumerates the row directly).

days.title: Proboscidea Volcanium
days.essence: Maximise pressure released in 30 minutes by choosing which valves to open while moving through a tunnel graph, after collapsing it to shortest paths between useful valves; the state space is best searched as a DP over a bitmask of opened valves. Part 2 adds an elephant working in parallel, splitting the valve set between two agents.

days.title: Pyroclastic Flow
days.essence: Simulate Tetris-style rocks falling under a repeating jet pattern and measure tower height. Part 1's 2022 rocks can be simulated directly, but part 2's one trillion rocks forces detecting a repeating (rock, jet, surface-profile) cycle and extrapolating the height.

days.title: Boiling Boulders
days.essence: Count exposed faces of unit cubes in 3D by subtracting shared faces between adjacent cubes. Part 2 wants only the exterior surface, requiring a flood fill of the surrounding air to exclude trapped interior pockets (the user solved part 1's total surface area).

days.title: Not Enough Minerals
days.essence: For each robot blueprint, maximise geodes cracked in 24 minutes by deciding which robot to build each minute, a branch-and-bound search needing aggressive pruning (cap robot counts, bound by best-case remaining geodes). Part 2 raises the limit to 32 minutes for the first three blueprints, blowing up the search further.

days.title: Grove Positioning System
days.essence: Mix a circular list by moving each original number forward/back by its value, where moves are taken modulo length-1 and duplicate values force tracking elements by identity. Part 2 applies a large decryption key and mixes ten times, demanding the modular-shift insight to stay fast.

days.title: Monkey Math
days.essence: Evaluate a tree of monkeys that either yell a number or an arithmetic operation on two others. Part 1 resolves root's value bottom-up; part 2 turns root into an equality and solves for the unknown 'humn' by inverting operations down the dependency path (the user solved part 1).

days.title: Monkey Map
days.essence: Walk a turtle around an irregular 2D board following move/turn instructions with wraparound. Part 1 wraps flat across the map edges, but part 2 folds the net into a cube so edges join in 3D, making the cube-face adjacency and orientation mapping the brutal core of the puzzle.

days.title: Unstable Diffusion
days.essence: Elves spread out on an infinite grid via a propose-then-move rule with a rotating order of direction checks, moving only if their proposed cell is unique. Part 1 counts empty ground after 10 rounds; part 2 runs until no elf moves, so the simulation must run on a sparse unbounded grid.

days.title: Blizzard Basin
days.essence: Find the fewest minutes to cross a valley while blizzards move cyclically, so the obstacle field is time-dependent; BFS over (position, time-mod-period) states is the key. Part 2 requires three legs back and forth, chaining the searches.

days.title: Full of Hot Air
days.essence: Sum numbers given in balanced base-5 SNAFU (digits 2,1,0,-1,-2) and convert the total back to SNAFU. The trick is the carry handling when encoding decimal into the balanced-quinary representation; the traditional finale with a single star.
